mnemonics: Most Pages Never Have Incredible Data
keyPoints: A page table is a data structure that maps virtual addresses to physical addresses.
It is used to manage the physical memory of a system.
The page table structure typically includes the following fields:
Page number, Page offset, Table index, Base address, Valid bit, Dirty bit, and Next frame number.
The page table is usually divided into frames, where each frame is a contiguous block of memory.
The page table is typically stored in main memory, but it can also be stored on disk.
The page table is used to handle address translation, page replacement, and page fault detection.
In a multi-programming environment, the page table is used to manage multiple processes and their respective memory spaces.
The page table structure can vary depending on the operating system and its architecture.
